name: Grappling Hook
description: Grappling Hook is a locomotion tool that helps the player cross space, reach anchors, or move through level structures.
design_notes: Grappling Hook should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For traversal mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use grappling hook as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect grappling hook to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels capable of crossing spaces that first appeared unreachable.

parameters.name: travel_speed
parameters.description: Movement speed while using the traversal mechanic.

parameters.name: activation_range
parameters.description: Maximum distance to a valid anchor, target, or route.

parameters.name: cancel_window
parameters.description: Time during which the player can safely cancel or redirect the action.

edge_cases: Grappling Hook is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Grappling Hook overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Grappling Hook runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Grappling Hook state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Grappling Hook plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Grappling Hook produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Grappling Hook strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Grappling Hook becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Grappling Hook with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Grappling Hook as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Grappling Hook with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Grappling Hook as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Wind Waker
example_games.note: Uses hook-style traversal to cross gaps.

example_games.title: Titanfall 2
example_games.note: Uses grapple movement for high-speed repositioning.
